title: Goru Rainstealer

{{Quote|It's Goru something, not sure of the name. He went into hiding a long time ago. He used to be some kind of hotshot weapons designer before those problems started. Apparently, both sides of this war wanted a piece of him. Anyway, I think he's the only one in the galaxy who can build the Zicx bug-bomb… What's the Zicx… uh, I probably shouldn't have said anything. Just forget it okay. Trust me, it's more trouble than you want to deal with.|A conversation about Goru Rainstealer that was overheard in a Narmle cantina|<ref name="Bug Bomb Quest" />}}
'''Goru Rainstealer''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] weapons [[Engineer/Legends|designer]] who designed the [[Zicx-bug bomb]], an illegal [[Grenade/Legends|grenade]]-like weapon made using [[Zicx]] [[Bug (creature)|bugs]] and [[Sarlacc/Legends|sarlacc]] bile. Due to his poor treatment of his clients and his illegal weaponry, Rainstealer earned the enmity of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]], [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]], and a [[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] named [[Valarian/Legends|Valarian]]. Due to the fact that these factions either wanted his work or wanted him [[Death/Legends|dead]], Rainstealer attempted to hide in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Narmle (city)|Narmle]] on [[Rori/Legends|Rori]], a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. However, at one point after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Rainstealer was approached by a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] who wanted him to produce a special weapon. Eventually Rainstealer consented and sent the spacer to procure the ingredients, which he used to produce several Zicx-bug bombs in order to keep that individual from speaking openly about him.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Oh no! They sent you to kill me didn't they?! I knew that those bloodthirsty thugs would catch up to me some day.|Goru Rainstealer, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG" />}}
[[File:ZicxBomb.jpg|thumb|left|183x183px|Rainstealer created the Zicx-bug bomb.]]
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] individual, Goru Rainstealer was a skilled weapons [[Engineer/Legends|designer]] who created weapons such as the [[Zicx-bug bomb]], which was a [[Grenade/Legends|grenade]]-like weapon made from live [[Zicx]] [[Bug (creature)|bugs]] suspended in [[Sarlacc/Legends|sarlacc]] bile. At some point during his life, Rainstealer's poor treatment of his clients angered both the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the two major factions in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. His creation, the Zicx-bug bomb, was made illegal by the Empire and was viewed as a repulsive weapon by the Alliance. However, various criminal factions, such as the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]] and [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]], wanted to obtain the weapon from Rainstealer. Altogether, Rainstealer's treatment of his clients resulted in both the Empire and Alliance wanting him [[Death/Legends|killed]], while the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]], Black Sun, and a [[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] named [[Valarian/Legends|Valarian]] also wanted him dead. At some point after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] during the Galactic Civil War, Rainstealer was living in [[Narmle (city)|Narmle]], a [[City/Legends|city]] on [[Rori/Legends|Rori]], a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. There, Rainstealer lived in fear that someone who he had angered or who wanted his work would come and kill him.<ref name="SWG" /> Rainstealer's status as a disgraced weapons designer was known to at least one [[Unidentified individual (Narmle cantina)|individual]], who was overheard by a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] while talking about Rainstealer and the Zicx-bug bomb in a cantina.<ref name="Bug Bomb Quest" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
While Rainstealer was living in Narmle, the spacer approached him with a request for a special weapon. Although Rainstealer was at first afraid the spacer was an [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] sent to kill him, the individual soon extinguished those fears. Rainstealer agreed to provide the individual with a Zicx-bug bomb, under the condition that the spacer keep quiet about Rainstealer's whereabouts. Although Rainstealer still had the casings for the weapons, he needed the spacer to bring him Zicx bugs and sarlacc bile for the weapon's active ingredients, which were only found on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. To gain access to these items, he gave the spacer his calling card and sent the individual to a friend, a [[Xenobiologist/Legends|xenobiologist]] named [[Jowir Ar'Lensa]]. Ar'Lensa helped the spacer acquire the necessary ingredients, at which point the spacer returned to Rainstealer in Narmle. Now that he had the needed components, Rainstealer upheld his side of the deal and built five bug bombs for the spacer. After building the bombs, he made it clear that he would not agree to make anything else, and due to his fear of assassins, Rainstealer reminded the spacer to keep his location secret.<ref name="SWG" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|It's ready, now! Here you go… Remember—you don't know me. You've never heard of me… and you definitely did not get this special weapon from me.|Goru Rainstealer, giving a spacer a Zicx-bug bomb|<ref name="SWG" />}}
Goru Rainstealer was a skilled weapons designer,<ref name="SWG" /> believed by some to be the only individual capable of constructing the illegal Zicx-bug bomb.<ref name="Bug Bomb Quest" /> During what he termed his youth, Rainstealer treated his clients in an unethical manner that angered various powerful organizations to the point where they wanted to kill him. However, while his weapons were viewed as unethical by the Empire and Alliance, they attracted the interest and envy of both Black Sun and the Hutts. The animosity from these organizations caused Rainstealer to live in fear of being attacked and killed by individuals working for them. He had a friend named Jowir Ar'Lensa, who was willing to help the individual that Rainstealer sent to him. Rainstealer had a large amount of knowledge about Zicx-bugs and had a high opinion of the bomb he made from them. His creation, the Zicx-bug bomb, was viewed as an infamous weapon<ref name="SWG" /> that was more trouble than anyone wanted to deal with.<ref name="Bug Bomb Quest" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
When he offered to build Zicx-bug bombs for the spacer, Rainstealer cautioned the individual to never mention him to anyone else due to his fear of the organizations he had angered. Upon the completion of the bug bombs, Rainstealer told the spacer that he could not make any more of the weapon, out of fear that he would be discovered.<ref name="SWG" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:GoruRainstealer.png|thumb|left|100px|Possible Human appearance]]
[[File:GoruRainstealer-Bith.jpg|thumb|right|100px|Possible Bith appearance]]
Goru Rainstealer was a non-player character in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], a [[Star Wars video games|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Rainstealer was located in the city of Narmle on Rori, where he would give players a quest to obtain ingredients for the Zicx-bug bomb. This required that the player go to Tatooine in order to work for Jowir Ar'Lensa in a settlement called [[Wayfar/Legends|Wayfar]]. After obtaining Valarian [[Spice/Legends|spice]] for Ar'Lensa, the player was awarded with a jar of Zicx bugs. The player then had to embark on a quest for a woman named [[Palu Zerk|Palu]], which involved the player rescuing Palu's sister, [[Feinu Zerk|Feinu]]. Completion of this quest yielded a protective coverall, allowing players to approach the sarlacc inside of the [[Great Pit of Carkoon/Legends|Great Pit of Carkoon]] unharmed. After extracting the bile, the player then needed to return to Rainstealer in order to receive five Zicx-bug bombs.<ref name="SWG" />

Goru Rainstealer was also mentioned in the "[[10 Hot Spots Travel Guide]]," an article on the game's website written by [[Michael Farone]] that featured an anonymous quote about Rainstealer and the bug bomb quest. In the "10 Hot Spots Travel Guide" article, Rainstealer appeared as a male [[Bith/Legends|Bith]],<ref name="Bug Bomb Quest" /> but Rainstealer was later given a permanent Human male appearance in the game.<ref name="SWG" /> Because the ''Galaxies'' feature is an official source,<ref name="Bug Bomb Quest" /> it cannot be concluded with absolute certainty whether Rainstealer was a Bith or Human. Although the player could choose not to do Rainstealer's quest, this article assumes 100 percent game completion.<ref name="SWG" />
